options.label: build
options.description: Changes that affect the build system or external dependencies (example scopes: gulp, npm)

options.label: chore
options.description: Changes to auxiliary tools and libraries (i.e. configs)

options.label: ci
options.description: Changes to our CI configuration files and scripts (example scopes: Travis, Circle, BrowserStack, SauceLabs)

options.label: docs
options.description: Documentation only changes (i.e. Markdown, code comments, docstrings, etc)

options.label: perf
options.description: A code change that improves performance

options.label: refactor
options.description: A code change that neither fixes a bug nor adds a feature

options.label: style
options.description:
* Changes that were only involving style sheets (CSS, SASS, LESS, etc)
* Changes that do not affect the meaning of the code (white-space, formatting, missing semi-colons, etc)

options.label: test
options.description: Adding missing tests or correcting existing tests

options.label: content
options.description: A change in the content that is visually displayed (i.e labels, text, copytext)

tokens.label: Scope
tokens.name: scope
tokens.description: A scope may be provided to a commit’s type, to provide additional contextual information and is contained within parenthesis, e.g., "feat(parser): add ability to parse arrays" (optional)

tokens.label: Short description
tokens.name: subject
tokens.description:
The subject contains succinct description of the change:

* Use the imperative, present tense: "change" not "changed" nor "changes"
* Don't capitalize first letter
* No dot (.) at the end

tokens.label: Body
tokens.name: body
tokens.description: Just as in the subject, use the imperative, present tense. The body should include the motivation for the change and contrast this with previous behavior. (optional)

tokens.label: Footer
tokens.name: footer
tokens.description: The footer should contain any information about Breaking Changes (optional)
